text_draft:
Pink Bear was covered in snow. Wet. Cold. Her pink fur matted.
But her button eyes gleamed.
Susie ran outside. She found the first egg nestled in a cleared patch of garden. Then another. And another.
The hunt was happening.
Pink Bear watched from the window, dripping snow onto the wooden floor. She wasn't out there searching. She didn't need to be.
She had done what mattered.
She had made it possible.
